definition.name: density_operator
definition.definition: A density operator (or density matrix) on a Hilbert space H is a positive trace-class operator ρ ∈ B(H) with Tr(ρ) = 1. When H is finite-dimensional (dim H = n), density operators are precisely the positive semidefinite n×n matrices with trace 1. The set of density operators is convex and compact (in the trace-norm topology for finite dimensions, or weak-* for infinite dimensions). Density operators are in bijection with normal states on B(H) via φ_ρ(A) = Tr(ρA). The rank of ρ, rank(ρ) = dim(range(ρ)), determines the GNS structure: the GNS representation of φ_ρ has commutant isomorphic to M_r(C) where r = rank(ρ), giving dim_R(commutant^{sa}) = r². Extreme points are rank-1 projections (pure states). Reference: Nielsen-Chuang, Quantum Computation and Quantum Information, §2.4.
